programmes.show_description: NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: Phase Free is a disaster preparedness concept that does not distinguish between everyday life and emergency situations - we also see how everyday items can be used in times of disaster.
Okayama / Hara Kenya (graphic designer). Hara visits a Japanese propeller maker with a 30% global market share, to discover the merit of finding an object's perfect shape, without compromise.
Explore the fascinating conventions and traditions of kabuki. Actor Kataoka Ainosuke explains the origins and meanings of kabuki's unique make-up, its onnagata techniques, and stage machinery.

Nagasaki after the atomic bombing transformed US combat artist Ted Gilien. His photos and paintings depicted the strength of the human spirit, and he devoted his life to creating art that opposed war.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Kumamoto Castle is one of Japan's most famous fortresses for its keep complex and elegant palace. In the 2016 earthquakes, it suffered serious damage, and multitudes are involved in its restoration.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Great Japanese music this week: NOA, an up-and-coming singer, dancer, and actor who trained in South Korea before debuting in Japan; and DEZOLVE, a young fusion band with dazzling virtuoso chops.
An animated series about a Japanese dog who gets lost in New York City. Charo says 'I want to see my owner again!', and so he starts his adventure to find his way back home. (S1, E02)
The waxing and waning moon has been loved, cherished, and given many special names throughout Japan's history - we explore the moon's continuing significance in agriculture, fishing, and daily life.
We take in Mount Fuji, Japan's tallest mountain, and meet some cats on the way - one called Bond carries out top-secret missions at a peach orchard as another patrols its 100-year-old winery home.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Yamagata is famous for its food, some of which is unique to late autumn and certain localities - our gourmet cyclist explores the regional cuisine from edible chrysanthemums to turnips and salmon.
A selection of acclaimed programmes from NHK's domestic channels dubbed into English.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Charleston, a historic East Coast port city founded in 1670, charms visitors with cobblestone streets, horse-drawn carriages, and rich history - it's loved by both international and US travellers.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Tottori's shiitake bento features thick, sweet and wood-grown mushrooms - rice seasoned with Japanese pepper pairs perfectly with the deep flavour of the shiitake enhanced by special local soy sauce!
Nishijin-ori - beauty synthesised in a Kyoto brocade. Dive deep into the beauty of the ancient capital which is still innovative 1,200 years after its foundation.
Accelerate your Japanese language skills! Learn practical and effective workplace expressions, understand business culture, and improve your kanji!
Wedding dresses with even slight damage often have to be discarded - Nogami Yuko uses these for upcycling and brings happiness from one bride to another with remakes that are better than new.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Two weeks after the Fukushima nuclear accident, a worst-case scenario was delivered to the prime minister - who would risk their lives to rein in the reactors? Key actors revisit those tense days.

A young man from Vietnam is using his own struggles finding an apartment in Japan to help other foreigners get settled - if you're new to Japan and face similar challenges, tell us.
This episode of a series on COP30 in Brazil focuses on Amazon 'dieback' - deforestation has reached 17 per cent and scientists warn that exceeding a figure of 20 per cent may disrupt the water cycle.

We visit a factory in Hamamatsu, where they use technology, hand craft, and finely tuned instinct to make keyboard harmonicas (a.k.a. 'melodicas') for everyone from schoolchildren to pro musicians.
Amami Oshima is a World Natural Heritage Site, with rare flora and fauna including many endemic species. Explore the biodiversity of its lush, subtropical forests in stunning time-lapse footage.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with pianist Mine Kawakami - for which she plays exclusive pieces - helps you experience each season.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: Phase Free is a disaster preparedness concept that does not distinguish between everyday life and emergency situations - we also see how everyday items can be used in times of disaster.
The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture - this episode looks at words related to birds. Explore the unique culture behind them.
#16 This is a world-famous hot spring. / Learn about the type of adjective that ends with 'na' and enjoy more ways to express your impressions about something.
A Sri Lankan student participates in the Mie Prefecture Youth Police Supporters volunteer programme that works with communities to build a safer society by preventing crimes before they occur.
Throughout April, NHK World-Japan marks 10 years since the Kumamoto Earthquake by showing related programs. Learn about efforts to restore Kumamoto Castle, and the Minami-Aso Luna Observatory.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
This programme visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Freight Expectations: This episode examines how agile logistics players are tackling the industry's toughest hurdles by breaking traditional norms and finding efficiency in unexpected places.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
In winter, seasonal workers arrive at mandarin orange groves for the two-month harvest. While living in communal lodgings, they share their views of the world, and plot new paths in life.
Chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto, and their cat Tama take us on a tasty journey of local cuisine served with a side of seasonal scenery at the Satoyama Kitchen guesthouse in Hida Takayama.

The Kinshicho and Kameido area is an urban subcentre in eastern Tokyo. We search for celebrated scenic views, savour local flavours, and learn how a network of canals evolved into a logistics hub.

Discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden in Uda - here the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule as a sanctuary of local culture and history.
A Bedtime Routine: On hot and humid summer nights, nothing can get in the way of sleep like mosquitoes - we learn about the tools Edo townspeople had at their disposal to fight these pesky insects.
Drug-resistant bacteria are infecting healthy people without being noticed, leading to what the WHO calls a 'silent pandemic.' We look at what we can do to protect ourselves.
